title: Nanomaterial Pilot Production
entry_mechanism: PSAP Track 6 — KACST-SABIC nanomaterials programme with international university research partnership
executive_summary:
Nanomaterials — materials engineered at the nanoscale (1–100 nanometers) with extraordinary properties — are the enabling frontier for multiple Saudi Arabia technology ambitions: conductive inks for printed electronics (SC programme), nano-coatings for aerospace structures (MA/SS), advanced catalysts for petrochemical efficiency (Aramco), and nanoparticle drug delivery for medical applications. Saudi Arabia currently imports all nanomaterials used across these sectors.

Node AM-4 targets establishment of a Saudi nanomaterials pilot production facility — not full-scale manufacturing initially, but the R&D-to-pilot scale that creates the foundation for commercial production in Phase II. SABIC's materials science infrastructure and KACST's research capability provide the platform; international nanotechnology companies provide the technology transfer.

The global nanomaterials market was valued at $12.8 billion in 2024 growing at 15.3% CAGR. Saudi Arabia's ability to produce carbon nanotubes, graphene oxide, silver nanoparticles, and nano-alumina from domestic feedstocks (SABIC carbon-rich streams, Ma'aden alumina) creates a cost-competitive advantage if the processing technology can be established.

companies.name: OCSiAl
companies.rationale: OCSiAl is the world's largest single-wall carbon nanotube producer with international production partnerships.

companies.name: Nanocyl
companies.rationale: Nanocyl produces MWCNTs with established industrial applications in polymer composites.

companies.name: Applied Graphene Materials
companies.rationale: AGM produces graphene nanoplatelets and has technology licensing partnerships.

companies.name: Strem Chemicals / Nanomaterials
companies.rationale: Strem supplies research-grade nanomaterials and has pilot production technology transfer capabilities.

companies.name: Umicore
companies.rationale: Umicore produces specialty materials including nano-catalysts and has Gulf industrial customer relationships.

partners.name: KACST Advanced Materials / Nanomaterials Research
partners.current: Carbon nanotube research; nano-composite development; some graphene research
partners.upgrade: GADD-KACST nanomaterials pilot facility; OCSiAl or Nanocyl technology transfer; Saudi carbon nanotube production from SABIC carbon-rich feedstocks; pilot to commercial transition

partners.name: SABIC Research and Innovation
partners.current: Materials innovation research; polymer additives research; some nanomaterials work
partners.upgrade: SABIC nanomaterials R&D investment; feedstock supply for Saudi nano-production; SABIC polymer + nanomaterial integration (e.g. carbon nanotube-reinforced polyethylene)

partners.name: KAUST Chemistry and Materials Science
partners.current: Nanomaterials research; surface chemistry; some 2D materials research
partners.upgrade: KAUST-KACST nanomaterials research programme; graphene and TMD research for electronics applications (SC sector); nano-coating research for aerospace (MA sector)

partners.name: Aramco Research Centre
partners.current: Enhanced oil recovery materials; catalysis research; some nanomaterials for reservoir applications
partners.upgrade: Aramco anchor customer for nano-catalysts from Saudi facility; dual-use oil/gas and advanced manufacturing nanomaterials
